J. G. Sapp, Miss Jamieson's mys-
teriOus and ardent wooer, will re-
main in jail till Judge Mann inves-
tigates some phases of the case.


The funeral of Richard Traul,
who shot c. II. Sawyer and then
killed himself last Monday, was
held this afternoon at O'Clock.


Myrtle Bacon. age four, ColviIIe,
Wash. is being treated at the Dea-
coness hospital for lacerated eye
ball, cut by a stick thrown by a



H. A. Thompson and his wife
were both slightly hurt yesterday
afternoon by being hit by a trolley
" hich fell from a Traction car.


Dealers in second hand goods
must report all purchases to the
police, according to the latest or
ders issued.


On complaint of Miss Anna Good,
Standpoint, the police are searching
for her tiance, A. G. Bourret, who
disappeared from the Imperial
hotel


Thomas Perrott, charged with
selling fake Masonic degrees, began
mis- trial in the police court yes
terday.


Elmer johnson, Garrett hotel,
says that his pocket was picked of
$40 while he was walking on Main
avenue last night.


Lady Sholto Douglas was sent to
laii for 80 days yesterday afternoon
on a vagrancy charge by Justice
Mann.


Mrs. Beatrice Miller, age 40, har-
assed b marital troubles. was saved
from poisoning by carbolic acid by
DR.. O'Shea last night, at E12
Sprague.


Dr.. L. L. Garrigues, Spokane,
has been appointed a member OF
the state board of medical eXami-
nerS.


Threshing reports from over
Spokane county are most flatter
ing. The yields will average over
% bushels to the acre.


The new First Pentecosta
Church of the Nazarene, Wall an(
Nora avenue, will be dedicated
Sunday afternoon at 8 O clock. The
building cost $4,000 and the par
sonage $4,000 more.


MA mems league for the aid Of
suffrage was formed last night in
room $50 the Hyde building. AL
fred H. Brown of Boston, is lead
ing in the movement.


r. /. King, travelling man from
Seattle, says the senatorial race
is between PoindeXter and Burke
He favors Burke, but thinks the
chances for PoindeXter are good
King says, Look out for a legis
lative frame up to beat the will Of
the people in case PoindeXter is
nominated"


Costanzo Coppula, 4-year-old sor
of l.. Coppula, died late Tuesday
night at the home of his parents
E37 Front avenue. Funeral serv
ices were held yesterday Afternoon
at o'clock.


Mrs. Amelia Vauer, age 48 years
died Tuesday at Medical Lake.


The funeral of Homer M
Daugherty, 20, who died Tuesda,
at the Deaconess hospital from in
juries received in the logging
camp near Elk, August 2. wa
held this morning at II o'clocl
from the Gilman undertaking
rooms. Burial will take place a
Greenwood.


The funeral Of Willhelmina Har
son. age 81 years, who died Tues
dav at her home, 01321 Normandi
street. will be held Friday aftel
noon at 9 o'clock from the res
dence and burial will take place a
Paramount.
